title: Unsupported-chain overlay leaves underlying controls keyboard-accessible
reasoning: When the wallet is connected to an unsupported chain, ChainGuard visually dims the protected page and blocks pointer events, but it still renders the original focusable controls. `aria-hidden` only hides the subtree from assistive technology; it does not remove links, buttons, or form fields from sequential keyboard focus. A keyboard user can tab into and activate protected page actions while the UI indicates the page is blocked for an unsupported network.
reproduction: Connect on an unsupported chain, load a page wrapped by ChainGuard that contains a button or link, press Tab until focus reaches an underlying control, then press Enter or Space. The underlying control can still be activated despite the unsupported-chain state.
recommendation: Use the `inert` attribute on the dimmed content, conditionally render a non-interactive placeholder instead of children, or recursively disable known actionable descendants while unsupported. Keep the warning and switch buttons outside the inert region.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests were included for keyboard navigation or unsupported-chain behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Render ChainGuard with a connected unsupported chain and a child button, simulate keyboard tab/activation, and assert the child action is not invoked while the chain-switch buttons remain usable.
